name: Levodopa
summary: Levodopa has strong indication-specific human evidence for Parkinson's motor symptoms, but supplement/herb attribution should not be generalized beyond L-DOPA-driven preparations.
description: Oral pharmaceutical dopamine precursor used clinically for Parkinson's disease; Mucuna-related evidence should be kept attribution-specific.
contraindications: Long-term clinical use can involve dyskinesia and dopaminergic adverse effects
usually co-administered with carbidopa in clinical practice.
conditions: not enough human evidence for a consumer recommendation
buying_criteria: Do not promote as a general consumer product
keep as research/reference unless human supplement evidence and safety context are added
